content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

In Chapter 4, we built our portfolio’s React showcase component and debugged Vitest unit tests inside the sandbox. Now, we want to look at content workflows: transcribing audio dev log vlogs and generating styled assets.
Because Antigravity CLI is multimodal — handling audio, video, and images alongside code — it can manage this entire asset pipeline. In this chapter, we will transcribe an audio dev log vlog (screencast.wav) and generate blog posts using the agent.
Spoken Audio Transcription
We prompt the agent directly inside the TUI:
Transcribe @screencast.wav and save the output as transcript.txt.The agent handles python environment setups (using UV manager), writes transcription scripts, calls Whisper, and generates output files autonomously. Review the sandbox confirmations to authorize file creation.
Clipping highlights via FFmpeg
Next, we want to extract a short audio clip highlighting our Figma MCP integration. We tell the agent to analyze the transcript and cut the file:
Read @transcript.txt, find the section discussing Figma MCP, and cut that segment from @screencast.wav using ffmpeg. Save it as figma_highlight.wav.Blog Posts & Visual Graphics
To design styled header images for our marketing posts, add the Nanobanana plugin:
/plugin add nanobananaNow prompt the agent to build the articles and style our illustrations:
Write a markdown blog post based on @transcript.txt. Take screenshots of our ManojGArc portfolio grid, run them through Nanobanana to generate stylized graphics, and link them in the post.Practice Materials & Resources
To follow along with the examples in this article, you can access the reference repository containing starter configurations, sample data, and prompts here.
